title: Fatal: NULL returned without an exception set in `_Py_CheckFunctionResult` (`call.c:43`)
description: `_symtable.symtable()` parses source via pegen; on a failed first parse pegen runs a second error-recovery pass that calls `PyErr_Clear()` (`Parser/pegen.c:917`/`928`, and the second `_PyPegen_parse`). Under OOM that path clears the live `MemoryError` and then fails its own allocation, so the C call returns `NULL` with no exception set, tripping `_Py_CheckFunctionResult`'s debug `Py_FatalError`.
notes: Generic 'returned NULL without setting an exception' invariant, fired through the symtable/pegen path. _symtable.symtable() (Modules/symtablemodule.c:66) -> _Py_SymtableStringObjectFlags -> _PyParser_ASTFromString -> _PyPegen_run_parser (Parser/pegen.c:939). On a failed first parse pegen does a heavy second error-recovery pass (reset_parser_state_for_error_pass + _PyPegen_parse + _Pypegen_set_syntax_error + _PyPegen_set_syntax_error_metadata); under OOM these allocations raise MemoryError, which the recovery code PyErr_Clear()s (pegen.c:917/928) while trying to upgrade the diagnostic, then fails its own alloc and returns NULL with no exception. faulthandler dumps a MemoryError with refcount 1 and EMPTY repr (normalization/repr could not allocate) -- the cleared exception. This is a Py_FatalError gated on #ifdef Py_DEBUG inside _Py_CheckFunctionResult (call.c:40-43): aborts on BOTH debug builds (ft_debug_asan and jit, Py_DEBUG=1) at start=4; compiled out on release builds (ft_release, upstream, Py_DEBUG=0), where the same defect instead yields a silent SystemError return rather than a clean MemoryError (latent correctness bug). Sweeping start 1..200 on the release builds raised only clean MemoryErrors and never aborted, so non-debug builds recorded n/a. Unlike OOM-0003 this is NOT FT-specific (jit/GIL build also aborts). Fix: preserve a pending non-SyntaxError exception across the error-recovery pass (save with PyErr_GetRaisedException before reset, restore if recovery set nothing; restore exc on the !metadata branch of _PyPegen_set_syntax_error_metadata instead of PyErr_Clear()); optionally backstop with PyErr_NoMemory() in _symtable_symtable_impl when a build helper returns NULL without an exception. 4 vehicles (python-5 x1, python-7 x3), all identical site via symtable.symtable (Lib/symtable.py:26) from symtable.main (Lib/symtable.py:412).
